pull_request.title: WRAPX-388: Fix Workflow Slug Generation After Organization Acronym Changes
pull_request.body:
Fixes https://yg-hpw.atlassian.net/browse/WRAPX-388

# Context

Workflow publishing could fail after an organization acronym changed away from a previously used acronym and then changed back. Existing workflow slugs could already be ahead of the active sequence, so publishing would generate a taken slug and fail instead of recovering to the next available value.

# Implementation

1. Make workflow publishing assign the generated slug and open status in one update so publish persists the final state atomically.
2. Recover from workflow slug collisions by syncing the active sequence to existing workflow slugs and retrying with the next available value.
3. Keep organization acronym updates aligned with the active workflow sequence so renamed organizations do not leave the sequence prefix or next identifier behind.
4. Move workflow alpha-numeric sequence conversion into the shared converter namespace and add inverse conversion coverage.
5. Keep the PubSub publish regression coverage current for the combined slug/status update.

# Screenshots

N/A — this PR changes backend workflow publishing, organization sequence handling, and API tests. It does not change product UI.

# Testing Instructions

1. Run the targeted API test suite:
   `./bin/dev test api -- --run api/tests/services/workflows/publish-service.test.ts api/tests/features/workflows/publish-service.test.ts api/tests/models/workflow.test.ts api/tests/services/organizations/sequences/determine-next-identifier-service.test.ts api/tests/services/organizations/update-service.test.ts api/tests/queries/workflows/build-workflow-with-highest-slug-query.test.ts api/tests/utils/converters/convert-to-numeric-sequence.test.ts`
2. Run the API typecheck:
   `./bin/dev api npm run check-types`
3. Boot the app via `./bin/dev up`.
4. Log in to the app at http://localhost:8080.

## Test Case 1: Workflow Publish Recovers from Stale Sequence State

5. Use an organization with existing workflow slugs for its current acronym where the active sequence is behind those slugs.
6. Publish a draft workflow for that organization.
7. Verify the workflow opens successfully and receives the next available workflow slug instead of failing on the stale generated slug.

## Test Case 2: Organization Acronym Updates Keep Sequence State Aligned

8. Rename an organization acronym to a value with existing workflow slug history for the current fiscal year.
9. Verify the organization sequence prefix matches the new acronym.
10. Verify the organization sequence next identifier is not behind the highest existing workflow slug for that acronym.
